Question: Algebra uses letters to represent numbers.
The letters in algebra are called variables.
The number part in an algebraic expression is called coefficient.
x + y + z is an algebraic expression.
A term is a part of an algebraic expression separated by + or -
A monomial has only one term.
x + 3y and 3y + x are like terms.
x + y and y + z are like terms.
x + y + z = y + x + z
The sum of like terms is always like term.
The sum of unlike terms is an algebraic expression.
A constant term cannot have a variable.
Algebraic expressions can have negative terms.
x + y + z ≠ x + z + y
